steps.title: Find the graphed line
steps.content:
The graph shown is $y=f(x)+14$. From the graph, two clear points on the line are $(0,2)$ and $(4,1)$, so the slope is

$$\frac{1-2}{4-0}=-\frac{1}{4}$$

The graphed line has equation

$$y=-\frac{1}{4}x+2$$

steps.title: Undo the vertical shift
steps.content: Since the graphed line is $f(x)+14$, subtract $14$ to get $f(x)$. $$f(x)=-\frac{1}{4}x+2-14$$ $$f(x)=-\frac{1}{4}x-12$$ The answer is A.
